_comment: Consumed by tools/security/secret-scan.py and the 'secret-scan' job in
Each allow[] entry suppresses a KNOWN, reviewed match (a test fixture or a documented
false positive). EVERY entry MUST carry path, scope, reason, owner, expiry
(YYYY-MM-DD), and rules. 'path' is an fnmatch glob relative to the repo root; 'rules' is
a REQUIRED, non-empty list of the SPECIFIC detector ids the entry may suppress. ['*']
(or any entry containing '*') is REJECTED at load time — never a blanket mask — so that a
NEW provider secret added to the file is NOT silently suppressed but surfaces as a
violation (fail closed, mirroring the SCA suppression validator). An EXPIRED entry stops suppressing —
the match re-surfaces and fails the scan — so waivers cannot rot. A real leaked secret must

allow.reason: Intentional non-functional fake-credential fixture that proves the secret detectors fire on every run; contains no real secret. 'rules' is scoped to exactly the two detectors the fixture carries so a NEW provider secret (e.g. a github-token) added here would FAIL the scan instead of being masked.
allow.reviewCriteria: On expiry, re-confirm the fixture is still fake and still needed; remove the entry if the in-repo scanner is retired. If a detector is added to the fixture, add its rule id here (deliberately).
